$comment: C23 _BitInt(N) in control flow (D-CSUBSET-BITINT), C1 — the coverage gap that let the first `I_BitIntWidthInconsistent` verifier miscope ship. (a) a _BitInt PHI: a `_BitInt(8)` ternary (a CFG merge, debug) + a `for (unsigned _BitInt(8) i; …)` induction var (mem2reg, release) each materialize a `_BitInt(8)` Phi — the denylist verifier's `instOperands(Phi)` ABORTS (a hard compiler crash). (b) a mixed return/arg-width CALL: `low4` returns `_BitInt(4)` from a `_BitInt(40)` arg, so the Call is `_BitInt(4)`-typed with a `_BitInt(40)` operand — the denylist FALSE-REJECTED it. The verifier now allowlists only the arithmetic ops the wrap chokepoint produces (Phi/Call exempt). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the allowlist to the `!conversion` denylist and this aborts (the Phi) / fails the build (the Call) instead of exit 42. Runs on debug AND release: 17 (ternary) + 15 (loop sum 0..5) + 10 (1034 mod 16) == 42.
